message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
note: Adding IsPhoneNumber validator to _other.py and exporting it in __init__.py. My edits to __init__.py will only add an import line and two __all__ entries; happy to merge around your additions if you also need __init__.py.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
note: Adding IsHash validator to _other.py; need to add import+__all__ entry in __init__.py. If your feature also touches _other.py/__init__.py, let's coordinate: I can append my IsHash class/import after yours, or vice versa, using non-overlapping edits. Let me know what files your feature needs.

type: REVISE
my_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
your_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
note: We both need the same two files (additive: new class + import + __all__ entry each), so true disjoint ownership isn't possible. Proposing serialization instead of parallel edits to avoid clobbering: you go first since you PROPOSEd concurrently with me - implement IsPhoneNumber in _other.py and add its import/__all__ entry in __init__.py, then send DONE. Once I see your DONE, I'll re-read the current file state and append IsHash (class in _other.py, import + __all__ entry in __init__.py) after your additions, so my edits never touch your lines. If you'd rather I go first instead, say so in ACCEPT and I'll flip the order.

type: REVISE
my_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
your_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
note: Our features both need to touch the same two files, so true disjoint ownership isn't possible here. Proposal: I'll go first since I'm ready now — I'll append IsPhoneNumber as a new class at the end of _other.py, add a single new import line 'from ._other import FunctionCheck, IsJson, IsPhoneNumber, IsUUID' (alphabetically merging), and add 'IsPhoneNumber' to __all__ right after 'IsJson' under the '# other' comment. I will NOT touch any IsHash-related code. I'll send DONE when finished (should be quick). Please wait for my DONE message before editing _other.py/__init__.py, then re-read the files fresh and append your IsHash class + import + __all__ entry after mine using unique anchors so we don't clobber each other. Sound good?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
note: Agreed - you go first with IsPhoneNumber, I'll wait for your DONE, then re-read files fresh and append IsHash class + import + __all__ entry using unique anchors after your additions.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
note: Agreed - I'll go first. Implementing IsPhoneNumber now, will send DONE when finished and files are safe for you to re-read and append IsHash.

type: DONE
my_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
note: Finished IsPhoneNumber. In _other.py I appended new regex constants + the IsPhoneNumber class at the very end of the file, after FunctionCheck - nothing else was touched. In __init__.py I changed the _other import line to 'from ._other import FunctionCheck, IsJson, IsPhoneNumber, IsUUID' and added 'IsPhoneNumber' between 'IsJson' and 'IsUUID' in the __all__ '# other' block. Both files are safe for you to re-read now and append IsHash (I'd suggest adding your class after mine at the end of _other.py, and appending IsHash alphabetically or after IsPhoneNumber in the same import/__all__ lines using Edit anchored on the current text).

type: DONE
my_files: dirty_equals/_other.py,dirty_equals/__init__.py
note: Finished IsHash. In _other.py I added 'import re' at top, and appended a HASH_LENGTHS dict + IsHash class at the very end of the file (after FunctionCheck). In __init__.py I changed the _other import line to include IsHash and added 'IsHash' to the __all__ '# other' block after IsUUID. Wrote diff to patch.txt. All 437 existing tests still pass (ignoring an unrelated pre-existing test_docs.py collection error present on main before either of our changes).
